title: Midday
commentary: The bulls are stampeding—Nasdaq surges +1.5% to session high 22,852, S&P +0.9% at 6,877, Dow +0.7% as risk appetite roars back post-Iran. Crypto is on fire: Ethereum explodes +8.9% to $2,158 intraday high, Bitcoin +4.5% near $71,300 on massive spot buying. Gold and silver consolidate gains (+0.5% and +0.7% respectively) but play second fiddle to the risk rally. Oil retreats -0.7% to $74 as geopolitical fears fade; dollar slips -0.25% to 98.81. The Iran panic has flipped into a full FOMO rally—no fear in sight.

title: Morning Session
commentary: Risk-on momentum builds into the morning—S&P +0.2% at 6,832, Nasdaq leads +0.5% at 22,620, Dow +0.2% as the post-Iran recovery extends. Gold consolidates gains +0.8% at $5,165, silver +0.5% at $83.88 after yesterday's volatility; metals holding but not surging. Crypto the standout performer: Ethereum rips to $2,108 intraday high (+6.4%), Bitcoin +3.2% near $70,500 as risk appetite returns. Oil flat at $74.68 (+0.2%); dollar slips -0.1% to 98.93. The Iran premium continues to unwind—buyers in control across asset classes.

title: Market Open
commentary: Precious metals stage a furious bounce at the open—gold surges +1.4% to $5,197, silver rips +2.8% to $85.78 as bargain hunters pile into yesterday's carnage. Equities flat to start: S&P 500 unchanged at 6,817, Dow flat at 48,500, Nasdaq steady at 22,517 as traders digest the Iran-driven volatility. Oil retreats -1.3% to $73.58 as Strait of Hormuz fears ease further; dollar slips -0.2% to 98.88. Crypto joins the recovery bid—Bitcoin +1.9% near $69,500, Ethereum +3.5% at $2,050. The metals reversal is the story: from yesterday's $400 gold crash to today's snap-back rally.

title: After Hours
commentary: Precious metals carnage deepens into the close—gold craters -3.7% to $5,115, silver obliterated -6.9% to $82.76 as Monday's safe-haven panic gives way to brutal profit-taking. Equities salvaged dignity from session lows: S&P -1.2% at 6,800, Dow -0.8% at 48,500, Nasdaq -2.0% as tech underperforms. Oil holds gains at $73.84 (+3.7%) with Middle East tensions simmering; dollar strengthens +0.6% to 99. Crypto drifts lower—Bitcoin -1.4% at $68,208, Ethereum pressured near $1,950 (-4.5%). The gold reversal is the story: from $5,394 high to $5,005 low, a nearly $400 intraday swing.

title: Market Close
commentary: Brutal reversal in precious metals steals the close—gold crashes -3.3% to $5,134 and silver hammered -5.7% as profit-taking hits safe-haven trades hard after yesterday's panic bid. Equities staged a remarkable recovery: Dow closes -0.6% at 48,603, having clawed back 1,600 points from session lows; S&P -1.2% at 6,800, Nasdaq -2.0%. Oil pares gains to +2.1% at $72.73 as Strait of Hormuz fears ease; dollar strengthens +0.7%. Bitcoin holds $68,443 (-1.0%), Ethereum weaker at $1,950 (-4.4%). The message: Iran selloff created a buying opportunity, not a bear market.

title: Afternoon Recovery
commentary: The comeback accelerates—Dow now down just 350 points at 48,552 (-0.7%), having recovered nearly 900 points from morning lows; S&P 500 -0.8% at 6,826, Nasdaq -0.9% as buyers gain conviction. Oil retreats to $80 (+7%) from $84 highs as initial Hormuz panic fades; Treasury yields ease off peaks. Crypto continues to stabilize—Bitcoin at $68,661 (-0.4%), Ethereum $1,989 (-2.1%) with selling pressure exhausted. Russell 2000 -1.3% underperforming as small caps struggle but well off worst levels. The tape suggests investors are treating today's Iran selloff as a buying opportunity, not the start of a new bear market.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Recovery gains steam—Dow now down 'only' 600 points at 48,305 (-1.2%), paring losses by half from morning lows; S&P 500 -1.3% at 6,789, Nasdaq -1.5% as defensive buyers emerge. Headlines shift from panic to opportunity: McDonald's, Alcoa flagged as buy-zone candidates amid the carnage. Oil holds at $82 (+8.5%) but retreats from morning highs; Treasury yields stabilize. Crypto consolidates recovery—Bitcoin at $68,295 (-1.3%), Ethereum $1,981 (-2.9%) finding floor after forced liquidations. VIX drifts back to 25; the dead-cat-bounce-versus-opportunity debate rages into the afternoon.

title: Midday Bounce
commentary: Dip buyers emerge from the wreckage—Dow claws back 400 points from session lows to 48,221 (-1.4%), S&P 500 recovers to 6,789 (-1.3%), Nasdaq -1.4% as tech finds footing. The worst of the morning panic subsides but markets remain deeply red: Russell -2.4%, DAX -3.6%, oil holding at $83 (+9%). Crypto stages a partial recovery—Bitcoin bounces to $68,320 (-1.9%), Ethereum $1,986 (-3.8%) off earlier lows as forced liquidations slow. Treasury yields ease from highs as some haven flows return; VIX retreats from 30+ but remains elevated at 27. The question: is this a dead cat bounce or dip-buying opportunity?

title: Selloff Deepens
commentary: The bleeding continues—Dow now down 1,200 points at 47,806 (-2.2%), S&P 500 -2.2% at 6,734, Nasdaq -2.4% as Iran attacks spread beyond initial targets and fears of regional war mount. Oil rips to $84 (+10%) as Strait of Hormuz shipping halts; Treasury yields surge with 10Y pushing 4.5% on stagflation fears. Global equities in freefall: DAX -4.0%, Russell 2000 -3.4%, TSX -3.6% as nowhere proves safe. Crypto joins the liquidation cascade—Bitcoin crashes to $67,590 (-2.2%), Ethereum hammered to $1,966 (-4.0%). Circuit breakers being watched; VIX screaming above 30.

title: Morning Selloff
commentary: Full capitulation underway—Dow craters 1,100 points to 47,789 (-2.3%), S&P 500 -2.1% at 6,737, Nasdaq -2.2% as fresh U.S. strikes on Iran spark fears of prolonged conflict. Global rout intensifies: DAX -3.8%, FTSE -3.2%, Russell 2000 -3.2% as risk assets flee for the exits. Oil surges past $82 (+8%) on Strait of Hormuz closure threats; Treasury yields spike as inflation fears reignite. Crypto joins the carnage—Bitcoin slides to $66,574 (-0.7%), Ethereum $1,942 (-1.8%) as Monday's safe-haven bid evaporates. VIX explodes above 28; no dip-buyers in sight.
